title: bromus hordeaceus subsp. longipedicellatus
intro: Bromus hordeaceus subsp. longipedicellatus, also known as long-bearded meadow brome, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the long-bearded meadow brome is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
